taskbody.context:
Use this procedure to assign guaranteed CPU and memory resources to the vRouter DPDK pods.
The Cloud-Native Router vRouter is integrated with the Kubernetes CPU Manager. You can use the Kubernetes CPU Manager to allocate dedicated CPUs to the vRouter DPDK pods.
Before you start, ensure you've enabled CPU Manager.

step.cmd: Specify the resource limits and requests for the contrail-vrouter-kernel-init-dpdk and the contrail_vrouter_agent_dpdk containers.

substep.cmd: Locate the charts/jcnr-vrouter/values.yaml file in your installation directory.
Edit that file to specify the resource limits and requests for both the contrail-vrouter-kernel-init-dpdk and the contrail_vrouter_agent_dpdk containers.
substep.info: resources: limits: cpu: <number_of_cpus> memory: <memory> requests: cpu: <number_of_cpus> memory: <memory>To guarantee that each container gets what it's asking for, set the same cpu value in both the limits and requests sections, and set the same memory value in both the limits and requests sections for each container.

step.cmd: Configure the helm chart to specify the number of guaranteed vRouter CPUs.

substep.cmd: Disable the core mask method of assigning CPUs by commenting out the following lines:
Configure the vRouter DPDK pods to use the guaranteed CPUs reserved by the CPU Manager.
substep.info:
guaranteedVrouterCpus: 5This value must be:
greater or equal to the number of CPUs configured for the contrail-vrouter-kernel-init-dpdk and the contrail_vrouter_agent_dpdk containers in charts/jcnr-vrouter/values.yaml, and
smaller or equal to the number of CPUs reserved by the CPU Manager.

step.cmd: Proceed with your Cloud-Native Router installation.
After Cloud-Native Router is installed, check to see how the CPUs are allocated.

substep.cmd: SSH into a node where Cloud-Native Router is running.
Look at the CPU Manager state.
